title: eriophorum russeolum subsp. rufescens
intro: Eriophorum russeolum subsp. rufescens, also known as red-tinged cotton grass, is a plant species in the family Cyperaceae, characterized by its bright red-tinged stems and fluffy white seed heads. The roots and rhizomes of red-tinged cotton grass have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues due to their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The plant's roots are believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. Eriophorum russeolum subsp. rufescens can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and its rhizomes can be dried and used in tincture or capsule form to harness its medicinal properties.
